2606.23205.title: The Correct Answer Trap: Pedagogically-Grounded Detection and Feedback for Hidden Misconceptions
2606.23205.paper_summary: Automated feedback systems that rely on answer correctness will reinforce, rather than address, misconceptions when students reach the correct answer through flawed reasoning. We investigate automatic detection of these hidden misconceptions using 20,964 real student responses from the Eedi mathematics platform. Fine-tuned classifiers detect only 57% of these hidden misconceptions, and standard ML interventions do not improve on this. An open-weight reasoning model detects 84%, but at realistic prevalence, false alarms outnumber genuine detections roughly 8 to 1. We present a graduated assessment rubric that separates answer correctness from method validity, and propose a detect-verify-escalate pipeline that routes uncertain cases to diagnostic follow-up questions rather than directly to teachers. Two deployment modes adapt the pipeline: a teacher dashboard where the system filters a review queue, and an autonomous tutor where flags trigger low-cost formative follow-up.
2606.23205.paper_summary_zh: 自動反饋系統依賴於答案的正確性，當學生通過錯誤的推理達到正確答案時，將加強而不是解決誤解。我們使用來自Eedi數學平台的20,964個真實學生回應來調查這些隱藏誤解的自動檢測。微調的分類器僅檢測到57%的這些隱藏誤解，標準的機器學習干預並未改善這一點。一個開放權重的推理模型檢測到84%，但在現實的流行率下，誤報的數量大約是正確檢測的8倍。我們提出了一個漸進評估標準，將答案的正確性與方法的有效性分開，並提出了一個檢測-驗證-升級的流程，將不確定的案例引導到診斷後續問題，而不是直接交給教師。有兩種部署模式來適應這個流程：一個是教師儀表板，系統在那裡過濾審查隊列，另一個是自主輔導，當標記觸發低成本的形成性後續時。
2606.23205.author: Moiz Imran et.al.
